title: OpenAI launches new initiative to help find and patch open-source bugs
slides: OpenAI wants to fix open-source bugs. Can it?
It builds a system that finds vulnerabilities automatically.
Human reviewers still decide which patches are safe.
Your open-source project could get a free security audit.
Submit your repo today. Test if AI finds your bugs.
thread_text:
AI hunts your bugs.

OpenAI’s system already scanned public repos and found real flaws. It works.

But it lacks permission.

The AI can suggest a fix, but only humans hit “merge.” The code stays under review.

The catch: false alarms.

Early tests show the AI flags many harmless patterns. Reviewers still waste time.

Your turn to get scanned.

Submit your GitHub repo. The AI will run its analysis and send you its findings.

Will you let it into your project?

Drop your repo link in the replies. See what the AI misses.
video_script: OpenAI built an automatic bug finder.
It scans open-source code for vulnerabilities.
Your project can get a free audit.
Submit your repo and test it.

script: OpenAI built an automatic bug finder.
It scans open-source code for vulnerabilities.
Your project can get a free audit.
Submit your repo and test it.

instagram.caption:
Your open-source project might already have a bug that OpenAI’s new scanner just found.

OpenAI built an automated system that hunts for vulnerabilities in public code—and it’s already spotted real flaws. 🕵️  
But it only suggests fixes: a human must still review and approve every patch before it goes live.

For you as a developer, this could mean a free security audit—but also more false alarms to sort through.

Would you let an AI scan your repo—or do you trust it to see what it’s missing? 🔍

carousel_slides.description: OpenAI built a system that finds real vulnerabilities in open-source repos. It works.
The AI suggests patches, but only human reviewers approve them. Security stays safe.
Early tests show many harmless patterns flagged. Reviewers still waste time filtering.
Submit your project for a free AI security audit. It scans and sends findings.
Paste your GitHub repo in the replies. See if the AI finds your bugs first.
